# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.

Write secure code.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export async function middleware(req: NextRequest) {
  const token = req.cookies.get('auth-token');

  if (!token) {
    return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
  }

  try {
    const isValid = await verifyAuthToken(token);
    if (!isValid) {
      return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
    }
  } catch (error) {
    return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
  }

  return NextResponse.next();
}

async function verifyAuthToken(token: string): Promise<boolean> {
  // Implement your token verification logic here
  // For example, verify with a secret or an external service
  return true; // Replace with actual verification logic
}

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/protected/:path*'],
};
